behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack". Slot B: handoff boundary (dispatcher -> reviewer clients) across modules. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a per-role wiring/serialization mismatch can be missed because isolated function tests won’t exercise real module contracts. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is about invocation payload correctness, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real dispatch/orchestration modules with test doubles at reviewer endpoints that capture requests; assert all three calls receive identical document bytes and role-specific instruction packs mapped to the correct reviewer role.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live clause extractor identifies key terms". Slot B: model-output boundary (real LLM extraction quality). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, stubbed outputs will always pass and miss real misses on legal phrasing/entity extraction. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior names one reviewer step, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause extractor model on representative contracts and score extraction against adjudicated labels for parties/dates/obligations/penalties, with pass criteria on recall/precision bands.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation". Slot B: handoff boundary (config propagation into invocation payload). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, contract/key-mapping drift between config builder and invoker can be missed. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior checks parameter passing, not whether the model’s risk judgment is correct.
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-assessor call path with real config assembly + invocation code and a capturing assessor stub; assert policy fields, versions, and thresholds in outbound payload exactly match configured values.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live risk assessor flags language ... including indirect or euphemistic phrasing". Slot B: model-output boundary (semantic interpretation quality). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, fixed fixtures hide failures where the real model misses indirect/euphemistic risk language. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because this is a single reviewer’s judgment, not chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk assessor on curated clauses with direct and euphemistic risk expressions; evaluate detection performance against expert labels and require minimum true-positive rates by category.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly ... invocation". Slot B: handoff boundary (context propagation into compliance-call input). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, integration bugs in profile serialization/field mapping into the checker request can be missed. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is input plumbing, not compliance decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run compliance invocation path with real context-building modules and a capturing checker stub; verify jurisdiction code, regulatory profile ids, and rule-set selectors in outgoing request.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live compliance checker correctly determines". Slot B: model-output boundary (AI compliance judgment correctness). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, deterministic stubs cannot reveal misclassification of nuanced regulatory text. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior targets one reviewer outcome, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real compliance checker on contracts with gold regulatory outcomes across jurisdictions/profiles; assert minimum accuracy/recall on requirement-level pass/fail determinations.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "merge step preserves both assessments with attribution" for conflicting reviewer outputs. Slot B: handoff boundary (normalization/merge across independent reviewer result schemas). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, isolated merge logic may pass while real adapter/schema mismatches drop one side or lose attribution. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because correctness here is deterministic merge/orchestration given reviewer outputs, not judging model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merger through real reviewer-output adapters for extractor + risk outputs with intentional conflict on the same clause id; assert unified report retains both assessments and reviewer attribution fields.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "End-to-end orchestration ... dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge" on a real contract. Slot B: multi-step agentic boundary (multiple live reviewer model calls plus aggregation). Slot C: if lowered to Agentic, single-step model tests miss cross-step orchestration/race/collection defects that drop findings during fan-out/fan-in merge. Rejection gate: none; behavior text explicitly requires chained multi-reviewer agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline with all three live reviewers enabled on real contracts, then verify every finding present in each reviewer output appears in final merged report with no drops and correct attribution.
